  • Uses of SingleRuleApplicationInfo in de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy

    Methods in de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy with parameters of type SingleRuleApplicationInfo
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull String
    AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition.getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    @NonNull String
    BreakpointStopCondition.getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    @NonNull String

    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    @NonNull String
    ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition.getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    boolean
    AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition.shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
    boolean
    BreakpointStopCondition.shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

    DefaultProver.applyAutomaticRule(GoalChooser<Proof,Goal> goalChooser, StopCondition<Goal> stopCondition, long startTime, boolean stopAtFirstNonClosableGoal)
    Applies rules to goals using the active strategy until a stopping condition is met.
